steps.title: Rearrange and factor out the common factor
steps.content:
Starting with $5(x+7) = 15(x-17)(x+7)$, move all terms to one side:

$$5(x+7) - 15(x-17)(x+7) = 0$$

Factor out $(x+7)$:

$$(x+7)[5 - 15(x-17)] = 0$$

steps.title: Apply the zero product property
steps.content:
If a product equals zero, at least one factor must equal zero:

$$x + 7 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad 5 - 15(x-17) = 0$$

steps.title: Solve both equations
steps.content:
From the first factor:
$$x + 7 = 0 \implies x = -7$$

From the second factor:
$$5 - 15(x-17) = 0$$
$$5 = 15(x-17)$$
$$\frac{1}{3} = x - 17$$
$$x = 17 + \frac{1}{3} = \frac{52}{3}$$

steps.title: Find the sum of the solutions
